steps.title: Apply the distance equals speed times time formula
steps.content: For any journey, distance = speed × time. Walking distance = $2 \text{ mph} \times w \text{ hours} = 2w$ miles. Running distance = $6 \text{ mph} \times r \text{ hours} = 6r$ miles.

steps.title: Sum distances to form the total distance equation
steps.content: The total distance covered is the sum of walking and running distances. Since Lao covers a combined total of 10 miles, we have $$2w + 6r = 10$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
